title: calluna erica var. hirsuta
intro: Calluna erica var. hirsuta, also known as hairy bell heather, is a variety of the heather plant that is native to Scotland and Ireland. It is a low-growing, evergreen shrub with small, pinkish-purple flowers and hairy leaves, which are used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of health issues. The plant has been traditionally used for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and boost the immune system. Hairy bell heather can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to create remedies for respiratory issues, digestive problems, and stress relief.
